summary: The complete expression domain has at most sixteen operands and retained DP results are capped at the 1,001 answer values, while interval-set sizes vary non-monotonically with digits and operators, so three legal expression tiers cannot provide an honest polynomial scaling verdict.

workload_bound.source_constraint: The expression contains at most fifteen operators and therefore at most sixteen operands; submitted and retained plausible answers are bounded from 0 through 1000.

replacement_checks.evidence: There are at most M squared intervals and M splits per interval; deduplicated child sets each contain at most V = 1001 retained values, bounding combination work by O(M^3V^2), followed by one pass over A answers.

Authored cases cover correct-over-partial precedence, duplicate answers, zero multiplication, partial-credit groupings, invalid answers, and the maximum fifteen operators; the exact native source also has remote Accepted evidence.
